seo.title: Journal — ArdenNest | Moments from the Ardennes
seo.description: Updates, seasonal atmosphere and stories from ArdenNest. Discover what's happening at our holiday home in the Ardennes.

heading: Journal
subtitle: Moments, updates and seasonal atmosphere — occasionally, when it matters.
emptyState: Our first moments will appear here soon.

section1.heading: What you'll find here
section1.p1: Not a weekly blog or a marketing newsletter. The journal is a slow place where we share something now and then — a seasonal picture, a small change to the house, a tip from nearby, a moment we'd like to remember ourselves. Nothing more, nothing less.
section1.p2: In practice, the updates tend to come in four shades:
items: Seasonal atmosphere — how the Ardennes look different in March than in October, and what that means for your stay.
The house itself — small renovations, new finds, occasionally a change of name (Ferme du Château has been ArdenNest since this year).
Neighbourhood & discoveries — a new brasserie, a walk we did ourselves, a local address we trust. Browse the full [neighbourhood guide](/en/surroundings).
Practical — availability tips for long weekends, early arrival options, or why we prefer to take a certain period offline.

section2.heading: Staying in the loop
section2.p1: We don't send an automated newsletter. The easiest way not to miss anything is to follow us on Instagram (@ardennest.be) — every journal post appears there too, often with more images. For concrete questions about a stay, an email or a phone call is always faster than waiting for news here.
section2.p2: The journal is meant for those who already know ArdenNest or are considering a stay — a way to keep in touch with the [house](/en/property) between visits, and to see what's coming for your [next stay](/en/booking).

welkom-bij-ardennest.title: From Ferme du Château to ArdenNest
welkom-bij-ardennest.excerpt: Our house has a new name and a new website. The house hasn't changed — neither has the warmth.
welkom-bij-ardennest.body:
Some of you have been coming here for years. Others have just discovered us. Either way: welcome, and thank you for reading.
We wanted you to be the first to know: our house has a new name and a new website. Ferme du Château becomes ArdenNest. The house hasn't changed — neither has the warmth. But we felt it was time the outside looked as beautiful as what you experience inside.
On the new site, we call it [quiet luxury](/en/differentiators). Sounds fancy, but it's exactly what you already know: that morning when all 26 were sitting at the big table and the only sound was the coffee machine. The children gone all afternoon in the garden — coming back with dirty knees and the widest smiles. Cooking together in that kitchen, with that range cooker — feeling like a Michelin-starred restaurant for a moment.
To celebrate, we made a short film. Do you recognise it?
The house is ready. So are we. Are you?
On the new site you'll find a lot more than before: a [detailed page about the house](/en/property), a [neighbourhood guide](/en/surroundings) with our favourite addresses — from the village café to the best fries around — and this journal, where we share something now and then. Not a weekly blog. Just small updates, a glimpse of the season, sometimes a tip that's too good to keep to ourselves.
We're around the house a lot this spring. If you'd like to come and see it, [discuss a stay](/en/booking) or simply give us a call — don't hesitate. We almost always pick up. And for those of you who already have a stay planned: see you very soon. We're looking forward to welcoming you — under the new name, in the same house.
With warm greetings from Gedinne,
Bieke & Christophe
